mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with t-stat > 2.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates and recession probabilities rise, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.0%, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01 in post-2010 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand and construction sector activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day returns during tightening periods, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 and no significant directional bias.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent while inflation remains elevated, real borrowing costs surge and equity valuations contract, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of observations above the 5.0% threshold, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in total nonfarm payrolls coinciding with rising unemployment indicates labor market deterioration and weakening economic momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor weakness signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance, or positive returns occur in > 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding corporate revenue bases and economic strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no predictive relationship with 5-day returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below its 2017 baseline while inflation moderates, supply chain bottlenecks resolve and profit margins expand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative or flat in > 60% of instances where INDPRO is below 100, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in backtests.
